mnemonics: Three-Way Handshake: SYN, SYN-ACK, ACK (Synchronize, Acknowledge Synchronization, Acknowledge)
keyPoints: TCP uses a three-way handshake (SYN, SYN-ACK, ACK) to establish a reliable connection.
Sequence and acknowledgment numbers are used to track data packets and ensure reliable delivery.
A connection is terminated using a four-way handshake (FIN, ACK) from each side.
commonMistakes: Confusing TCP's connection-oriented protocol with UDP's connectionless nature.
Assuming the three-way handshake involves only two packets instead of three.
Forgetting that both sequence AND acknowledgment numbers are crucial for reliable data transfer.
